headline: A High-Margin SaaS Leader Poised for Growth

sections.Company Overview: Adobe Inc. is one of the largest and most diversified software companies in the world. The company offers a line of products and services used by professionals, communicators, businesses, and consumers for creating, managing, delivering, measuring, optimising, engaging, and transacting with content and experiences across various digital media formats. Business segments include Digital Media (73% of revenue), Digital Experience (25% of revenue), and Publishing and Advertising (2% of revenue). The company operates in the Americas (60% of revenue), Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) (25% of revenue), and Asia- Pacific (APAC) (15% of revenue) as of FY23. The company offers many of the products via a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) model or a managed services model through term subscriptions and pay-per-use.
sections.Investment Overview: To benefit from continuous digitalisation. Adobe has an early mover advantage with its SaaS transformation in 2009, achieving above 80% SaaS revenue since 2017. The SaaS transformation triggered robust revenue growth along with margin improvement. In 1QFY11/25, ADOBE achieved a 38% operating margin, which is among the highest of its peers. The company offers high growth opportunities riding on the trend that businesses will continue to adopt digital solutions. Equipped to capture the expanding addressable market. Adobe’s total addressable market is projected to be USD205bn in 2024, and reach USD293bn by 2027, according to the management. The company's consistent innovations and strategic acquisitions, complemented with the introduction of Firefly in March 2023, a platform offering a variety of AI tools, position Adobe strongly to capture an expanding market share. After several months of beta testing, Adobe has unveiled the commercial plan for Firefly, featuring a new credit- based system that allows users to access its tools with subscription packs starting at USD9.99 per month. Wide range of product offerings with high customer stickiness. Adobe offers a wide range of products with high value-add to both individual and corporate customers. Their Creative Cloud provides the tools necessary to create content in any format, Document Cloud contains its signature product Acrobat that helps customers to create fully digital workflows, and Experience Cloud offers a one-stop shop for businesses to sort their marketing, advertising, and analytics needs. Customer stickiness is also high for Adobe, which leads to more opportunities to upsell. BUY Adobe with a revised TP of USD660 (prev USD703). Our revised TP is based on 12.3x (prev 16x) forward EV/Sales, above its 5-year historical average of 11.7x, as we believe the commercialisation of its Firefly AI platform will fuel future growth for the company. Potential share price catalysts include (1) product price increase and (2) higher operating margins.
sections.Risks: Global economic uncertainty. An economic slowdown may lead to a reduction in enterprise spending on digital products. Intensifying competition. Adobe participates in a highly competitive environment globally with a wide range of competitors in various industry segments. Intensifying competition could create downward pressure on product pricing and margins. Analysts: Andy YU | dbsvhk@dbs.com Sachin MITTAL | groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Buckle on tight as US travel demand softens

sections.Company Overview: Airbnb, Inc. primarily operates a global platform for stays and experiences. The Company’s marketplace model connects hosts and guests online or through mobile devices to book spaces and experiences around the world. It partners with hosts throughout the process of setting up their listing and provide them with a range of tools to manage their listings, including scheduling, merchandising, integrated payments, community support, host protections, pricing guidance, and feedback from reviews.
sections.Investment Overview: Leading vacation rental marketplace: strong network effects strengthens economic moat. As at end-2022, ABNB had over 8mn global active listings and more than 5 mn hosts as at June 2024 which is telling of its strong brand position. The strong network effects are also evident in how prior guests remains the top source of hosts and guests also becomes hosts when they travel to other regions. We are of the view that ABNB’s position as a leading vacation rental marketplace will not be easily displaced given the strong network effects which brings together a pool of hosts and guests. Room night growth and OpEx discipline to drive EBITDA growth despite moderating ADRs. Consensus sees gross booking value increasing at 11%/12% in FY24/25, driven by 12%/12% growth in nights and experiences booked and moderating ADRs of -2%/-1% in FY24/FY25. We see downside risks to ADRs given changes in mix towards lower ADR regions, recently introduced pricing and discounting tools, as well as normalisation of record room rates. With its unique business model connecting hosts and guests, ABNB has an operationally lean model with low overheads. ABNB continues to stay well-disciplined with costs, as seen by a 5% decline in headcount despite a 75% growth in revenue (2022 compared to 2019). Accordingly, EBITDA should come in 12%/15% higher in FY24/FY25, implying operating EBITDA margin of 36%/37% for FY24/FY25. Further moderations in 3Q24 expected on signs of tapering demand in the US. We see room for downward revision in consensus forecasts (USD3.84bn) to be in line with 3Q24 revenue guidance of USD 3.67-3.73bn (+8-10% y/y). The group expects “sequential moderation” in growth of nights and experiences in 3Q24 as the booking window shortens and travel demand in the US slows. We believe that a contraction in the booking lead time is indicative of consumers’ lower propensity to spend on travel given the economic uncertainty. Despite expecting a modest y/y increase in ADRs in 3Q24, management is guiding for flattish adjusted EBITDA on a y/y basis in 3Q24 on higher marketing investment to support new growth markets (vs consensus growth of +9%). Overall, we believe that the risks are skewed towards the downside given slowing domestic travel in North America which accounts for the vast majority of nights booked within the region. Upgrade to BUY with unchanged TP USD165. We roll over our TP to 19x CY26 EV/EBITDA, which is close to -0.5SD of the historical mean. Despite tougher y/y comparisons expected in 2H25 due to the strong second half of last year, we believe the new verticals in services and experiences could become meaningful long-term contributors. Early adoption metrics for services such as high guest satisfaction, strong host interest, and seamless app integration indicate a credible path to scale and support long-term monetization potential. Consistent execution in expansion markets also confirms product market fit and brand success in new regions, which could help diversify revenue concentration risk outside North America.
sections.Risks: Macroeconomic slowdown. Recession and macro uncertainty may impact discretionary spending on travel and could negatively impact travel service providers through fewer bookings and rates. Intensifying competition. ABNB competes with both online and traditional travel service providers such as Booking Holdings and Expedia. Players such as Google are also expanding into the travel booking space by offering services such as Google Hotel Ads, Google Flights, and Google Travel, adding competitive pressure to the industry.

headline: Share pice catalysts in cloud & search

sections.Company Overview: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) is the holding company of Google, a tech giant offering a wide range of technological products in the likes of search engine, maps, calendar, mail, operating system. GOOGL also sells hardware products and online advertising services. GOOGL’s reporting segments are Google Services, Google Cloud, and Other Bets. In terms of global market share for search engine, GOOGL has 91%.
sections.Investment Overview: Generative AI will boost monetization across Google's ecosystem. In 3Q25, Google Services had a 38.5% operating margin with strong performance in Search (USD56.6bn), YouTube ads (USD10.3bn), and subscriptions/platforms/devices (USD12.9bn). Gen-AI tools and Shopping Graph's 35bn listings enhance ad monetization and drive online-to-offline commerce. With ~90% global search share, Services revenue is projected to grow at a 9% CAGR (FY26F–28F), maintaining digital ad dominance. GCP margins are improving rapidly. Cloud revenue grew 34% y/y to USD15.2bn in 3Q25, powered by strong uptake of AI Infrastructure and Generative AI among 70%+ of enterprise customers, yielding a 24% operating margin. Though below AWS and Azure, consensus sees GCP EBIT margins rising to 24–28% in FY26F–28F on USD77–128bn revenue (29% CAGR). A USD155bn backlog, converting 45–50% yearly, supports growth, while multi- year 2025 contracts monetize from 2026 amid elevated FY25–26 capex and continued capacity investments ahead. Ad-tech sentiment improved after Google halted the third-party cookie phaseout (April 2025). Chrome will introduce user-managed cookie settings, preserve data advantages and allow further Privacy Sandbox testing. Google advertising revenue was USD74.2bn in 3Q25, up from USD66bn in 3Q24. BUY with a revised TP of USD360 (prev USD323). Our TP is based on 33x (prev 30x) FY26F PE and FY26F earnings of USD135bn. The re-rating reflects: (i) rolling forward net profit base by 2 months, and (ii) higher target multiple of 33x vs previous 30x. Our multiple is at a 10% premium to peer average 30x as consensus expect GOOGL’s earnings to grow at a CAGR of 14% over FY26F- 28F vs peer CAGR of 12% as GOOGL is expected to gradually step up AI monetization in 2026 and rising margins of its cloud business.
sections.Risks: Macroeconomic slowdown, weaker ad or cloud margins, shifting mobile search behavior, competitive ad-tech disruption, and adverse regulatory outcome. Sachin Mittal Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: E-commerce poised for market share growth and margin expansion

sections.Company Overview: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) is an American multinational technology company which focuses on e-commerce, cloud computing, digital streaming, and artificial intelligence. It operates through three segments: North America, International and Amazon Web Services (AWS). AMZN is the leading e-commerce player globally and accounts 39% of the US ecommerce business. AWS which provide on-demand cloud computing platforms is also the global leader accounting 34% of the market share.
sections.Investment Overview: Amazon’s Retail segment (North America and International) continues its recovery. Amazon's retail segment is rebounding, posting a USD3.2bn operating income in 1Q25 with a 5.8% margin, nearing 4Q23’s record 6.1%. This turnaround is driven by reduced delivery costs from logistics regionalization. Amazon now holds a 42% U.S. e-commerce share, up from 41%, as the market is set to grow at a 12.3% CAGR through 2027. Analysts project an 11% retail revenue CAGR for Amazon, with margins rising to 3% in 2025 as fulfillment costs stabilize and Amazon Logistics expands. AWS Growth Softens Amid Macro Headwinds. AWS remains Amazon’s most profitable segment, contributing USD6.2bn in operating profit in 1Q25 despite slower 14% y/y revenue growth amid macro headwinds and cautious enterprise spending. Though it represents just 16% of total revenue, AWS drives profitability. The company continues investing in AI-powered cloud offerings, including generative AI and proprietary chips like Trainium and Inferentia. Expanding advertising stream with accretive margins. Amazon’s advertising segment continues strong growth, with 1Q25 ad revenue reaching USD11.5bn, up 21% y/y, outpacing peers like Snap (-3%) and Meta (+8%).With the digital ad market projected to grow at a 10% CAGR through FY26F, Amazon’s ad revenue is expected to grow 17%, increasing its market share to 13% by 2024 through its integrated retail media network. BUY AMZN with a higher TP of USD255 (prev USD235). Our TP for AMZN is based on 24m forward EV/EBITDA multiple of 15x (prev 13x) on FY26F EBITDA of USD191bn (prev USD157bn). The valuation multiple is at a 25% premium to the peer average of 12x. We have discounted the valuation by 10% annually. The higher TP stems from (i) rise in 24m forward EV/EBITDA peer average multiple from 11x to 12x, and (ii) Consensus projects 15% EBITDA CAGR for AMZN over FY24-26F, (vs. peer average of 11%). Our target price and higher multiples on AMZN are further strengthened by its leading position in e-commerce, and leadership in cloud services through AWS, hugely benefiting from Gen-AI and raising opportunities in the online advertising business.
sections.Risks: E-commerce could face supply chain disruption & a tight labour market, along with inflation & slowing consumer discretionary spending, all of which could weigh on growth & profitability. Furthermore, if top-line demand in the e-commerce business and/or AWS slows during a period of heavy long-term investments, could result in limited margin expansion. On the other hand, future regulatory action could pose additional risks too.

headline: Long-term growth remains intact

sections.Company Overview: New York-based American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is a leading global services company, primarily focusing on payment solutions and services. Among its principal products and services are charge and credit cards, payment network services and travel related services offered to consumers and businesses. AXP ranksasthe fourth largestcard network globally by purchasevolume,behindChina UnionPay, Visa and Mastercard, and boasted 140m cards in force worldwide as of end 2023.
sections.Investment Overview: Leading player in the high-end credit card market. American Express continues to dominate the premium credit card segment, connecting effectively with affluent consumers and SMEs through strong brand equity, superior service, and a lucrative rewards ecosystem. The recent U.S. Platinum card refresh has driven doubled account acquisitions and elevated engagement, supported by the Amex Travel App and enhanced digital tools for premium cardholders. Strategic partnerships with Toast, Resy, and Tock, along with the launch of Amex Ads, strengthen customer experience and open new monetization channels. Amex cards are now accepted at an estimated 160 million merchant locations worldwide, nearly 5x growth since 2017, reinforcing the company’s global network and competitive moat. Long-term growth remains intact. 3Q25 results demonstrated resilience, with revenue +11% YoY to USD18.4 bn, EPS USD4.14, and billed business USD421 bn, driven by robust premium card spending and engagement. Expansion in the high-end consumer and SME segments, adoption among younger cardholders, and rising offline travel and lifestyle spending support long-term targets of 10%+ revenue growth and mid-teens EPS growth. Operational efficiency and disciplined marketing investments further enhance sustainable profitability, while AXP’s #1 ranking in the J.D. Power 2025 US Credit Card Satisfaction Study underscores strong customer loyalty and brand strength. Caution remains on macro and competitive headwinds. Despite strong execution, risks include potential slowdown in T&E and discretionary spending, rising engagement costs, and intensifying competition from JPMorgan Chase and Citi in the premium card segment. Emerging credit risks in lower-income segments, though limited, and sensitivity to broader macroeconomic conditions could temper near-term growth. Nevertheless, high-income customer spending remains resilient, supporting billed business and fee growth. Maintain HOLD, TP raise to USD360. We maintain HOLD, raise TP to USD360, based on a 21x FY26F forward PER (previously 17x), reflecting a 22% discount to Visa. While long-term drivers - digitalization, premium engagement, and network expansion - remain intact, higher expenses, credit risk, along with lagging adoption in tokenization and digital payments, justify the discount. Strong YTD share gains (~23%) suggest much of AXP’s premium card resilience is already priced in, supporting a near-term HOLD stance.
sections.Risks: Upside risks include stronger-than-expected consumer spending and favourable economic conditions leading to improved credit quality. Downside risks include increased competition or regulatory actions concerning credit cards, particularly related to interchange rates, as well as deteriorating credit quality. Ken SHIH

headline: Phenomenal new products and technological barrier to support growth

sections.Company Overview: Apple Inc. (Apple) designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories and sells a range of related services. The company’s products include iPhone (51% of FY24 revenue), Mac (8%), iPad (7%), wearables, home, and accessories (9%) like AirPods, Apple TV, Apple Watch, HomePod, and others. The company also has an online services business (25%), which provides services like 1) operating platforms like the App Store and iTunes, which allow customers to purchase and download applications and digital content; 2) offering digital content through subscription-based services, including Apple Arcade, Apple Music, Apple News+, Apple TV+, and Apple Fitness+; and 3) providing a range of other services, such as AppleCare, iCloud, Apple Card, and Apple Pay.
sections.Investment Overview: iPhone specification upgrades to drive premium model mix higher. We expect the launches of the ultra-thin iPhone 17 Air in 2025 and Apple's first foldable iPhone in 2026 to set new benchmarks in the smartphone market. Android manufacturers will likely follow suit, accelerating their own specification upgrades. These innovative product introductions should significantly boost shipment growth momentum. Apple currently commands a dominant 71% share in the premium segment, supported by an established user base exceeding 150 mn, which provides an excellent platform for adoption of new innovations. The introduction of these cutting-edge iPhone models is expected to further enhance the premium model mix, driving earnings growth in FY9/25F as gross margins expand amid an accelerating replacement cycle. China Weakness. Apple's iPhone sales in China have declined by 17% y/y in 2024, affected by intense competition from local brands like Vivo, Huawei, and Xiaomi, who are capitalising on innovation and targeted subsidies. The gradual implementation of AI features has further delayed iOS user upgrades. Despite retaining leadership in the high-end market, Apple faces persistent challenges, with the outlook remaining subdued until the anticipated launch of the significantly upgraded next-generation iPhone 17/Air, featuring an enhanced AI Siri. The technology breakthrough in semiconductor design. Apple has made significant strides in semiconductor design, starting with the launch of its first ARM-based M1 chip in November 2020, moving away from Intel chips. The M1-equipped Macs offer superior performance, battery life, and heat efficiency compared to their Intel-based predecessors, driving a 31% increase in Mac sales in 4Q20. Apple has since expanded the M series chips to the iPad Pro and is expected to continue advancing its chip technology for iPhones, Apple Watch, and headsets, strengthening its competitive edge across product lines. We are HOLD on AAPL with a TP of USD210 per share. Our TP is based on 28.5x forward PE, 0.5 SD above its peers' average due to its strong user loyalty, which supports long-term product growth and higher shareholder return compared to its peers. AAPL’s share price has outperformed the NASDAQ year-to-date, and we believe the short-term drivers have already been factored in. We expect AAPL to achieve steady growth driven by the success of its promising premiumisation and leading product technology in the long run.
sections.Risks: Delays in Apple Intelligence launch could impact growth. Apple's in-house LLM is expected to launch sometime in 2026; any further postponement could adversely affect consumer sentiment and iPhone sales. Analyst: Jim Au | dbsvhk@dbs.com

headline: Net Interest Margin (NIM) set to turn around

sections.Company Overview: Bank of America offers a full range of banking, investment management and other financial and risk management products and services to individuals, small- and middle-market businesses, large corporations and governments. The business segments include Consumer Banking, Global Banking, Global Wealth and Investment Management and Global Markets. The bank commands a leading market share in retail deposits and small business lending domestically in the US.
sections.Investment Overview: Strong retail franchise; long-term strategy on focusing on responsible growth. Bank of America continues to be a market leader in the United States a strong retail franchise, having one of the highest shares of retail deposits and small business lending. Aside, Bank of America has a long-time strategy of driving responsible growth within the bank’s risk framework, culminating in a strong underwriting process and superior credit quality compared to its peers historically. Net interest income (NII) has turned the corner. Bank of America has lifted its guidance for 4Q25 NII (FTE basis) guidance to USD 15.6–15.7 bn (previous: ~USD 15.5–15.7 bn), representing approximately 8% y/y growth. Management also indicated that a 100 bps parallel downward shift from the September 30, 2025 forward interest rate yield curve would reduce net interest income by an estimated USD 2.2 bn over the next 12 months.While Bank of America interest-bearing deposit costs rose 2bps during 3Q25 (2Q25: -3bps q/q), total funding costs have been trending lower since 3Q24. Resilient asset quality shines through. With a high quality loan book across both commercial and consumer portfolios, Bank of America has consistently outperformed peers in the annual Fed stress test for the past five years to have the lowest potential loan losses in a severe downturn. We continue to like Bank of America for its strong asset quality, as the industry continues to see higher charge-offs from credit cards through 2024. Maintain BUY with higher TP of USD64, representing 15.0x FY26F P/E c. 2 SD above 10-year historical average. We believe that cost of equity is likely to be higher going forward. The company is currently trading at 11.8x FY26F P/E, representing 0.3 SD above its 10-year historical average. We believe that Bank of America will see more favourable growth trajectory ahead with better net interest margins ahead.
sections.Risks: Recessionary risks. Downside risks include recessionary risks associated with higher than expected inflation, higher-than-expected Fed rate cuts. We believe downside risks weigh higher due to the ongoing trade war. A sharper than expected slowdown in the macroeconomic environment may also lead to slower loan growth and higher delinquencies and credit losses. Jia Hui NG Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: A Scalable Compounder in the Era of Alternative Asset Dominance

sections.Company Overview: Blackstone Inc. is the world's largest alternative asset manager, overseeing over USD1.2 trillion in assets under management (AUM) as of Q2 2025. The firm specializes in providing investors with diversified exposure to private markets, offering investments across a wide range of strategies including private equity, real estate, private credit, infrastructure, and hedge fund solutions. Blackstone's core business model is built on generating recurring management fees and performance-related income. Its strategic approach involves making long-term, value- driven investments that are generally less correlated with public markets. The firm's significant scale, deep expertise accumulated over decades, and expansive global network are key competitive advantages. These attributes enable Blackstone to identify and access unique investment opportunities (proprietary deal flow) and effectively deploy capital across various economic cycles.
sections.Investment Overview: Leveraging scale for long-term market share gains. Blackstone Inc. stands as the world's largest alternative asset manager, commanding over $1.2 trillion in AUM as of Q2 2025. Its comprehensive offerings across private equity, real estate, private credit, infrastructure, and hedge fund solutions provide investors with diversified exposure to private markets, which are less correlated with public equities and bonds. This unparalleled scale, coupled with deep expertise and a global network, provides significant competitive advantages, enabling the firm to access proprietary deal flow and deploy capital across various economic cycles. Resilient-than-peers growth outlook. Blackstone offers a unique combination of earnings durability, secular growth, and capital efficiency. Its leadership in key alternative sectors— especially private credit, infrastructure (particularly AI-powered infrastructure), and real estate—positions it to benefit from long-term shifts in institutional and individual capital allocation. The expansion of its perpetual capital vehicles and increasing demand from private wealth channels enhance fee stability and visibility, ensuring a more resilient revenue stream compared to peers more reliant on traditional, public market strategies. Maintain BUY with TP of USD190. Blackstone's strong balance sheet, consistent dividend yield (approximately 3.3%), and proven ability to generate performance fees across cycles make it a compelling long-term compounder. As private markets increasingly become crucial for yield, diversification, and innovation exposure, Blackstone is positioned as a scalable, resilient, and high-quality platform for sustained outperformance. The "BUY" recommendation with a target price (TP) of USD190 reflects confidence in its continued leadership and growth trajectory in the alternative asset management space.
sections.Risks: Emerging risks. While Blackstone's business model is robust, its earnings are inherently tied to AUM and performance fees, making it sensitive to market cycles and asset valuations. The firm faces potential fundraising risks from disruptions in attracting both institutional and private investors. Furthermore, exposure to illiquid, long-term investments introduces valuation uncertainties and credit/default risks. Growing areas like digital infrastructure also present sector-specific risks, including rapid technological change and evolving regulations. Operational and geopolitical risks, including reliance on key personnel and navigating diverse global regulatory environments, also warrant attention. Analyst Vishal Kapoor| groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Clearer skies lie ahead beyond the near-term turbulence

sections.Company Overview: Boeing is a premier aerospace and defence company, recognised globally for its extensive range of commercial jetliners, and defence, space, and security systems. As one of the top exporters in the U.S., it supports airlines and U.S. and allied government customers across more than 150 countries. Its operations span three primary segments: Commercial Airplanes (44% of FY23 revenue), which produces the 737, 747, 767, 777, and 787 families, as well as Boeing Business Jets; Global Services (32%), providing MRO and aftermarket services for commercial and defence clients; and Defence, Space & Security (24%), which entails designing and manufacturing advanced rotorcraft, electronic and defence systems, missiles, satellites, and launch vehicles.
sections.Investment Overview: Despite recent challenges, a formidable presence in its core markets underscores enduring dominance and strategic resilience. Boeing holds a dominant position in the commercial aerospace sector, operating as part of a duopoly with Airbus. Together, they produce the majority of the world’s narrowbody and widebody aircraft, a market characterised by demand consistently outpacing supply. Despite recent production challenges, airlines are compelled to remain with Boeing, as Airbus’s production slots are booked into the next decade. In the highly regulated defence sector, Boeing is one of the top five prime contractors for the US and allied nations. With its extensive product range and deep expertise in driving innovation, Boeing is poised to capitalise on secular growth opportunities across both sectors. Earnings and cash flow inflection from 2HFY25, as production stabilises and deliveries accelerate. Boeing remains on track for recovery in FY25, with 737 production set to exceed 38 per month later this year, reaching 42 by year-end, while 787 output rises to 7 per month. The Spirit AeroSystems acquisition, tighter supply chain oversight, production streamling and process improvement should enhance execution, while inventory liquidation and the wind- down of shadow factories will support cash flow. Although 4QFY24 highlighted ongoing operational challenges, we believe increasing deliveries and a more stable production cadence will drive a turnaround in earnings and cash flow in 2H25, setting the stage for stronger financial performance. Improving free cash flow trajectory should drive a re-rating, despite near-term credit concerns. Boeing’s USD3.5bn cash burn in 4QFY24 reflects working capital pressures and production inefficiencies, but free cash flow should turn positive in 2H25, as deliveries ramp up and supply chain bottlenecks ease. While the USD10bn free cash flow goal will likely be pushed to FY27, we expect progressive deleveraging from FY25 onwards, easing credit overhang concerns. Execution risks remain, particularly in defence contract overruns and regulatory approvals for the 737 MAX, but with demand fundamentals intact and improving operational stability, we see meaningful upside as Boeing regains investor confidence through sustained cash flow recovery. We rate Boeing a BUY with a TP of USD260.0. Our TP is based on 25x FY27 EPS (discounted back), which is around 2SD above its 5-year pre-crisis average, and at a moderate premium to the sector average of 20-22x. We justify this premium based on Boeing's recovery from a low base, coupled with its strong positioning for sustained growth into the next decade, contingent on successful execution.
sections.Risks: Ongoing 737 MAX production issues risk delivery delays and cash flow slippage. Tariff-related cost increases could erode margins if mitigation proves insufficient. Analyst Jason Sum | groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Accelerating AI momentum

sections.Company Overview: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) designs, develops, and supplies a range of semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions through two segments: semiconductor solutions and infrastructure software. Its semiconductor solutions segment includes Internet protocol (IP) licensing and provides solutions for managing the movement of data in data center, telecom, enterprise, and embedded networking applications. Major end markets include broadband, networking, wireless, storage and industrial.
sections.Investment Overview: Cementing market leadership through inorganic growth. Broadcom specialises in the design/development of semiconductors in the communications and connectivity segments and operates as an infrastructure software provider. This is enabled through a series of successful mergers, acquisitions, and integrations over the years, e.g., merger between Avago and Broadcom, and acquisitions of CA Technologies and Symantec Enterprise Security. Successful integration of VMWare should further bolster its position in the infrastructure software space. Broadcom differentiates itself through its high-performance design and integration capabilities and focuses on developing products for target markets, with above industry margins. Twin engine of AI-chips demand and VMware acquisition. We see Broadcom as a key beneficiary of the ongoing AI-boom as a key custom AI-chips player, with opportunities in both the near- and long-term. Management expects AI-chips sales to accelerate further to USD6.2bn in 4QFY25 (+19% q/q, +66% y/y), with near-term order visibility through the recent addition of USD10bn custom AI chip order from a fourth cloud customer, which is expected to ship over next year. Further out, Broadcom sees an addressable market for AI market to reach between a range of USD60-90bn by FY27, as 3 of its hyperscalers clients remain committed to each deploy AI clusters containing 1 million AI custom chips by 2027. Improving financials underscore right strategic direction. Broadcom's successful integration of its acquisitions is evidenced in its improving financials. The group registered a 10-year CAGR of 28% for revenue and 35% for EBITDA. The shift towards software - with stickier customers and low tendency to switch - has helped to improve margins. Within the semiconductor space, cyclical weakness in broadband and server storage is offset by network’s accelerating AI-driven growth. Moreover, Broadcom's track record of generating consistent free cash flows, and paying stable dividends to its shareholders, are also strong differentiating factors vs peers. We have a BUY recommendation on Broadcom with a higher TP of USD390 (from USD330), pegged to 18X blended FY25F/26F P/BV (or +2.25SD vs 5-year average). We like Broadcom as the leader within the custom AI chips value chain. Twin growth engines through accelerating AI chips momentum and stable VMware contribution remain intact, with further support coming through the gradual recovery of the non-AI semiconductor segments. Near-term momentum finds good support on robust order visibility and pipeline underpinned by 1) record consolidated backlog at USD110bn, 2) the addition of the USD10bn custom AI chip order from a fourth cloud customer, and 3) the sustained shift towards low-latency, inferencing needs. This is reflected in consensus’ optimistic forecast for AI semiconductor revenues to average >10% q/q growth over the next 8 quarters.
sections.Risks: Risk of non-accretive acquisitions. Relatively high leverage. Slowdown in AI growth momentum.

headline: Multiple growth drivers in place for 2026

sections.Company Overview: Chevron Corporation is one of the world's leading integrated energy companies and is the second largest oil company based in the US, behind ExxonMobil. The Company operates in both the upstream and downstream segments. Chevron produced around 3.3mmboepd of oil & gas in 2024, of which around 48% was produced in Chevron’s home market – USA.
sections.Investment Overview: Earnings to remain robust in near term. Chevron's strong earnings profile and cash flows, together with its efficient cost strategy core, are expected to continue in the future. The upstream segment is the main growth driver. Chevron has also recently announced plans to develop scalable power solutions for U.S. data centers. Kazakh project ramps up, Hess acquisition completed finally. CVX's project pipeline, including the Anchor project, Whale platform in Gulf of Mexico and Kazakhstan Future Growth Project (FGP), supports long-term production growth, with Kazakhstan volumes driving significant free cash flow accretion potential in 2026. Meanwhile, the successful acquisition of Hess provides Chevron with immediate access to a high-quality rapidly expanding asset in Guyana, which will improve Chevron's production profile and growth rates well into the next decade, potentially leading to a re-rating of the stock as the market fully digests the implications of this prized asset. Strong cash flow profile and low gearing are Chevron's key strengths. Chevron has historically generated better free cash flow metrics compared to the industry average, with gearing lower than peers. Chevron remains focused on superior cash returns to shareholders, with quarterly share repurchases for 2026 guided at unchanged rate of USD2.5-3.0bn depending on market conditions. We have a BUY rating on Chevron with a revised TP of USD190. Our TP is based on a 2.1x forward P/BV multiple, which is at a slight premium to earlier valuations following its successful acquisition of Hess, which should be transformative to Chevron's growth profile. Chevron is also the only US player currently active in Venezuela and sees the potential to grow production volumes materially over the next 18 to 24 months.
sections.Risks: Chevron's earnings depend on the price volatility of crude oil, natural gas and other refinery products. Delays in startup of key projects and cost overruns are some other risks to look out for. Suvro Sarkar Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Peer leading major packaged food & beverage player

sections.Company Overview: The Coca-Cola Company is a global total beverage company. It owns or licenses and markets various beverage brands. Key sparking non-alcoholic beverages are Coca-Cola, Sprite, Fanta, Schwepps, while other brands include Powerade, Dasani, FuzeTea, Costa Coffee, and Minute Maid. Its global unit case volume mix split in FY24 is 16% US, 33% Mexico, China, Brazil and India, and remaining 51% from other geographies. Overall, the company derives 59% of its revenue from concentrate and 41% from finished products; its concentrate operations also command significantly higher operating margins (est 40%-60%) than its finished products operations (6%-7%). The company has over 200+ brands worldwide and its products are available to consumers in more than 200 countries.
sections.Investment Overview: Transiting to be the world’s smallest bottler through franchise model. Coca-Cola operates a unique franchise distribution network, investing in international bottling partners through its Bottling Investment Group and later divesting its equity stakes once they become successful. It has over 950 bottling plants across 200 bottling partners worldwide. Post-divestment, these partners retain exclusive rights to bottle Coca-Cola products, paying a franchise fee and remaining contractually obligated to purchase concentrates from the company. This model ensures high-margin revenue while monetising bottling stakes. Maintained guidance for organic revenue growth of 5% to 6% and raised USD EPS growth from 2% to 3% to 3% for FY25. Management projects 5%–6% organic revenue growth and 2%–3% EPS growth for FY25. Growth will be price/mix-driven (3%–5%), while volume growth is expected at 1%–2%, aligning with consensus estimates. Price/mix impact is expected to moderate, given easing inflation in hyperinflationary markets and Fairlife’s slowing growth. Management is optimistic about volume, aiming for the upper end of guidance (2%). As of 1H25, the entire 5% organic growth was driven by price/mix with concentrate volume flat y/y while unit case volume was up 1% y/y. With strong profit growth in 1H25, the company plans to reinvest part of the gains into marketing initiatives. With broadly supportive underlying trends and stepped-up investment in 2H, management remains confident in accelerating volume growth in 2H25. Well-defined strategies in place to navigate headwinds and support continued growth. Coca- Cola has clear strategies to mitigate challenges and sustain growth. For instance, it used generative AI to create its 4Q24 Christmas ad, reducing costs and turnaround time. Regarding the 25% aluminium tariff, management expects a manageable cost impact through packaging adjustments (shifting towards PET over aluminium) and hedging strategies. On the balance sheet, net debt stands at 1.8x EBITDA, rising to 2.0x EBITDA post-Fairlife contingent consideration payment but remaining at the lower end of its 2x–2.5x target range. Share buybacks have been slow, with USD4.8bn in repurchase headroom, partly to support the Fairlife consideration payout and potentially prepare for an adverse IRS tax appeal outcome, which could take years to resolve. Lift TP to USD83 (prev: USD77). We applied a higher valuation of 28x, +3S.D. of its 5-year average forward PE, on its FY25F earnings. We believe the higher valuation premium is justified given its positioning as one of the few rare blue chip consumer staple names relatively insulated from current trade tensions with its highly localised production and well-executed all-weather strategy.
sections.Risks: Key risk includes uncertainty surrounding the IRS tax dispute appeal, but a resolution is likely years away.

headline: Good times continue for the independent oil major

sections.Company Overview: ConocoPhillips is an independent exploration and production company, headquartered in Houston, Texas and a key operator in the prolific Permian Basin in the US. Total production for 2024 amounted to 1.99mmboepd, up 8.5% y-o-y. The company’s production mix roughly consisted of 51% crude oil, 31% natural gas, 14% NGL, and 4% bitumen.
sections.Investment Overview: Leader in low-cost portfolio. ConocoPhillips (COP) continues to focus on generating low cost-of-supply portfolio by transitioning its well drilling, casing, and completion design. It has also undertaken property management to increase efficiency, as well as acquired businesses like Concho Resources and Shell’s Permian assets over the years to establish a leading position in the prolific Permian basin. With record production of 1.99mmboepd achieved in 2024, COP has guided for around 18–20% production growth in 2025 to 2.34– 2.38mmboepd, driven by organic growth as well as the completion of landmark acquisition of Marathon Oil in late 2024. Production guidance has been maintained at the end of 1H25, despite the planned USD1.3bn sale of Anadarko Basin assets, closing early 4Q25. Growing its core oil and LNG portfolios. 2025 capex budget has been moderated to USD12.3-12.6bn from c.USD12.9bn but this is still higher than 2024 levels of USD11.5bn, driven by continued growth in Alaska & Canada (USD2.0bn) and on track to delivering low single-digit organic production growth. Total company and Lower 48 production in 3Q25 reached 2,399mboepd (2Q25: 2,391mboepd) and 1,528mboepd (2Q25: 1,508mboepd) respectively, which reflects the Marathon Oil integration. The LNG strategy continues to scale, adding 5 MTPA of offtake for a ~10 MTPA portfolio, with North Field East, North Field South and PALNG on track for first LNG in 2026, while LNG project capital has been cut to USD3.4bn after a USD0.6bn credit against Port Arthur spend. Meanwhile, Willow (Alaska) capex estimates was raised by USD1.5bn to USD8.5–9.0bn range due to inflation, localised North Slope and marine cost escalation, which could be concerning to shareholders, given the project is already pricey. Though first oil from Willow was narrowed to early 2029, keeping the ~USD4.0bn FCF inflection intact, progress will be keenly watched here. Enhanced shareholder return guidance. COP has a strong balance sheet position and has committed to return at least 30% of its cash from operations to shareholders, with more usual 40-45% actual payout range. For 9M25, distributions have totalled USD6.9bn, on track to beat 2024 level of USD9.1bn. Distributions will roughly be split 40:60 between dividend and share buybacks. This implies close to 9.5% total distribution yield at current prices. Given the macro uncertainties, commitment to this level of distributions for the year will be a strong support for the share price. Preliminary 2026 guidance points to 0–2% underlying production growth, with capex at USD12bn (USD0.5bn below 2025 midpoint) and adjusted operating costs easing further (USD0.4bn lower y/y), supported by lower major project capital, Marathon Oil synergies, and cost reduction programme. We have a BUY call on COP with a TP of USD118. COP’s share price performance has been affected by recent volatility in oil prices. However, we reckon oil prices will hold up in 2H25, and given the strong earnings delivery and shareholder returns commitment, we reiterate our BUY rating on COP, with an unchanged TP of USD118. Our TP is based on a forward P/BV multiple of 2.3x, which is at +0.5 S.D. levels historically.
sections.Risks: COP is exposed to significant macroeconomic risks as earnings depend on the price volatility of crude oil, natural gas and other refinery products. Further, COP is exposed to significant legal and regulatory risk as energy industry is highly regulated in different jurisdictions across the world. The Willow project in Alaska has been mired in controversy owing to environmental concerns and it remains to be seen whether the development can progress without delays in future. Suvro Sarkar Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Rich and diversified project pipelines to fuel future growth

sections.Company Overview: Equinix, Inc. is a global data centre service provider catering to a diverse customer base across industries such as technology, finance, media, healthcare, and more. Equinix's operation reaches business hubs across the Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Middle East. In addition to its core data centre leasing business, the company also provides services like digital infrastructure, data exchange, and private connections to hypercloud providers, such as AWS and Microsoft Azure.
sections.Investment Overview: Global leader in the data centre industry. Equinix holds a c.21% in the global retail colocation market according to Synergy Research as of 2024, with 260 data centres in 33 countries across six continents. The company has 44%, 36%, and 21% of revenue coming from America, EMEA, and Asia-Pacific, respectively. It also has one of the most interconnected ecosystems, as it serves 10k+ customers and has 478k+ total interconnections within its facilities. Equinix partnered with Singapore's sovereign wealth fund- GIC - in July 2019 to develop hyperscale data centers under the " xScale " brand name. As of September 2024, Equinix had an xScale portfolio of 20 operational facilities across all three regions mentioned above. In 3Q24, Equinix announced plans to expand its xScale data centers through a USD15bn joint venture with CPP Investments and GIC, to build advanced, high-capacity facilities across U.S. campuses. Equinix added four new projects since 4Q24 across Frankfurt, Miami, Mumbai, and Washington, D.C., and opened 10 major projects, including capacity in Kuala Lumpur, Lagos, Manchester, Salalah, Santiago, and São Paulo. Equinix has a very good track record of growth and profitability. It has 20 years of consecutive revenue growth and sustained a 45%+ adjusted EBITDA margin in the past 10 years. Solid pipelines to fuel future growth. Equinix estimates that it will have 382k cabinet equivalent capacity by the end of FY24, compared to the current 364k total cabinet capacity. In terms of project pipelines, Equinix has more than 22,000 cabinets of retail capacity and more than 100 megawatts of xScale capacity to be delivered through the end of 2025. Diversified customer mix reduces exposure to macro volatility. Equinix's 10k+ customer base comes from a diverse set of industries, with 35%, 36%, and 29% of them from the cloud, enterprise, and network sectors, respectively. The company’s strength also stems from its unmatchable global reach, with 64% of its recurring revenue coming from customers deployed across all three regions. Its top 10 customers account for 17% of its MRR and top 50 customers account for 38% of its MRR. Monthly recurring revenue (MRR) increased by 3.8% y/y (flat q/q), in 1Q25, with the Americas region increasing by 4.1% y/y (flat q/q) and improvement across all regions. Moreover, the occupancy is bound to be very stable with very high lease terms – over 18 years on average and 83%+ of leases expiring after 2039. Rate BUY with unchanged TP of USD1,218. based on 29x 12-month forward EV/EBITDA, c.1 SD above 5-year historical average, as street expects Equinix EBITDA to grow by 11% CAGR over the FY24F-FY26F period and the continuous expansion of 56 major projects underway in 33 metros across 24 countries, including 12 xScale projects.
sections.Risks: Foreign exchange rate risk. More than 50% of Equinix's revenue is contributed by the APAC and EMEA markets. Customer in-sourcing. Hyperscale cloud providers – the largest existing tenants of data centres – may opt to move all or significant data centre servers in-house. Analysts: Andy YU | dbsvhk@dbs.com Sachin MITTAL | groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Eclipsed by industry top dog; near term pressure on core take rates

sections.Company Overview: Expedia Group,Inc. isan online travelcompany.TheCompanyhasthree segments. The Retail segment provides a full range of travel and advertising services to its worldwide customers. Its portfolio of retail brands includes Brand Expedia, Hotels.com, Vrbo, Orbitz, Travelocity, ebookers and Wotif Group. B2B segment encompasses its Expedia Partner Solutions business. Trivago segment generates advertising revenue primarily from sending referrals to online travel companies and travel service providers from its hotel metasearch websites.
sections.Investment Overview: One of the largest OTAs and is poised to leverage on a growing travel industry. Digitalisation has concurrently hastened the decline of traditional brick-and- mortar travel agencies whilst advancing the breakthrough of online travel agencies. According to Research and markets, the global online travel market size is expected to grow from US$475b in 2022 to US1009b in 2030, representing a CAGR of 9.88%. Behind BKNG, Expedia is still one of the largest OTAs which consensus expects to deliver EBITDA growth of 8%/10% in FY25/26, below Booking Holding's 12%/12%. Strategy targeting high LTV customers could pave the way for LT margin expansion; although long term marketing spend could weigh on near-term EBITDA. Expedia’s new customers turned loyalty members increased more than 60% and hit a record high in 4Q22. The focus on acquiring high lifetime value (LTV) customers could support margin expansion in the long term however higher marketing investments could weigh on near term margins. The street forecasts EBITDA margins of 21.9% for FY25, slightly higher than 21.4% in FY24 on the stronger effect of top line growth. A clear winner emerges between the two industry juggernauts: preference for BKNG over Expedia. Consensus forecast shows that Expedia’s EBITDA margins is on an upward trend and will range between 20-23% in FY24-FY26. However, this is still below Booking Holding’s EBITDA margins of 34%-37% in FY24-FY26. We believe that is mainly attributed to the market dynamics of the key geographies that the players operate in. Expedia mainly operates in the US where large hotel chains are predominant, resulting in lower bargaining power to charge higher commission rates. On the other hand, Booking Holdings operates mainly in Europe which comprises smaller hotels which gives more pricing power. We believe that it is hard for Expedia to play catch up with Booking Holdings as the profitability difference stems from geographic exposure. While it is not hard to venture into new markets, scaling operations require much more investments. For this reason, we prefer Booking Holdings over Expedia. HOLD Expedia with target price USD220 (vs USD160 previously). Our TP is based on c.8x CY26 EV/EBITDA, c.50% discount to the 17x EV/EBITDA that Booking Holdings is trading, and close to -0.4SD of the historical mean. The valuation reflects Expedia’s outsized exposure to the US market, which accounted for over 60% of revenue in FY24. Across regions, US remains the weakest geography with low single digit growth, in contrast to mid single digits in EMEA and mid teens in rest of the world. Nonetheless, we raise our TP to account for continued execution of cost discipline measures and growth of the B2B segment which powers offline retail and corporate travel.
sections.Risks: Macroeconomic slowdown. Recession and macro uncertainty may impact discretionary spending on travel and could negatively impact travel service providers through fewer bookings and lower rates. Intensifying competition. Expedia competes with both online and traditional travel service providers such as Booking Holdings. Players such as Google are expanding into the travel booking space by offering services such as Google Hotel Ads, Google Flights, and Google Travel, adding competitive pressure to the industry. Amanda Tan | Sachin Mittal

headline: Capitalise on market volatility

sections.Company Overview: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. is a global ﬁnancial institution that delivers a range of ﬁnancial services across investment banking, securities, investment management, and consumer banking to a diversiﬁed client base that includes corporations, ﬁnancial institutions, governments, and individuals. The company operates through three segments after its recent re-organisation, namely (1) Global Banking & Markets, (2) Asset & Wealth Management, and (3) Platform Solutions, which includes consumer and transaction banking businesses.
sections.Investment Overview: World-class broker with top talent. Goldman has consistently occupied the top spots of investment banking league tables and meaningfully expanded the wallet share of its top trading clients, thanks to its excellent culture and ability to attract top talent via a performance-driven compensation system. Going forward, the company would trim its principal investment balance sheet while (1) refocusing on its core banking and markets business, (2) growing its asset and wealth management fees, and (3) scaling its young platform solutions business with the aim of achieving profitability. Normalization in Market Activities and Disciplined Strategy Execution to Drive ROE. We believe Goldman Sachs is on track to sustain its medium-term ROE target in the mid-teens, with ROE of high-teens aﬃrming strong execution. This is driven by: (1) investment banking fee recovery driven by M&A, (2) continued exit of principal investments to free capital for redeployment, (3) Asset & Wealth Management to see AUS to grow at teens % with strength in alternative, and (4) a streamlined platform business, with a three-year efficiency plan enhancing profitability. Set to deliver resilient performance, with robust trading income counterbalancing potential delays in investment banking activities amid market volatility. We note record equities trading revenues and robust FICC revenues, showcasing the ﬁrm’s ability to capitalise on a “highly dynamic environment,” as clients repositioned portfolios. This strength, driven by leadership in derivatives and ﬁnancing, is likely to persist as volatility fuels trading demand, partially oﬀsetting softer advisory revenues (down y/y) and a cautious M&A outlook due to recession risks and trade policy uncertainty. With a diversiﬁed franchise, USD40bn buyback program, and eﬃciency gains from AI investments, Goldman is well-positioned to navigate delays in dealmaking while sustaining mid-teens ROE. BUY rating with TP of USD890. Our target valuation is based on 16x FY26F P/E, justiﬁed by 16% EPS CAGR during FY24-27F. The higher than historical average target multiple reﬂect expected ROE improvement from successful strategy execution, the improving M&A deal cycle, strong equity trading income amid market volatility, and sustained market momentum amid de- escalation of trade tension. We expect the company to maintain a mid-teens ROE through- the- cycle.
sections.Risks: Global economic slowdown, prolonged high market interest rates, noticeable contraction in market liquidity, and a substantial correction in global asset valuation. Ken SHIH | Edmond FOK

headline: ROE outlook outperforms peers

sections.Company Overview: JP Morgan Chase (JPM) is a leading global financial services firm, providing investment banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management services. The bank is one of the largest banks in the United States, with assets of over c.US$3.7 trillion, as it continues to gain market share across all business segments in the last decade. The business segments include Consumer & Community Banking, Corporate & Investment Bank, Commercial Banking and Asset Management.
sections.Investment Overview: Net interest income (NII) remains robust in 2025. Management further raised its FY25F NII to be higher at ~USD95.8bn (previous guidance: USD95.5bn). Similarly, guidance for FY25F NII excluding trading is further raised to USD92.2bn (previous: ~USD92bn) as balance sheet growth partially offsets lower rates. During the current cycle, JPM continues to outperform most peers as it delivers stronger-than-expected net interest income as JPM continues to gain deposit market share across the US. International expansion for retail business through online bank, Chase. While JPM has delivered positive market share growth across various operating segments in the last few years, we believe the success of international expansion for its retail business remains to be seen as it involves heavy execution and spending. Currently, Chase UK has acquired >2 million customers since its launch in 2021 and is aiming for profit in 2025 with JPM reportedly targeting to launch across several European countries in the medium term and expand its wallet share across more products. Keep watch on expenses and credit costs. JPM previously estimated for the international expansion for retail business to break even in 5-6 years, incurring ~USD1bn of losses over several years. FY25F operating expenses excluding Firmwide legal expense guidance have been further raised to an all-time high of ~USD95.9bn (FY24: USD91.1bn), representing 5% growth y/y and suggesting negative operating leverage as JPM continues to drive through various investments for growth. We continue to keep watch on consumer credit asset quality as management continues to expect higher credit losses in cards business into FY26F, alongside stress in commercial real estate sector. Maintain HOLD with revised TP of USD310. We maintain HOLD with revised TP of US310 post guidance revision, representing 15.0x FY26F P/E, c. 1.9SD above 10-year historical average. We believe valuations have started to turn expensive, given the share price run up through 1H2025. Management has unveiled a 17% through the cycle ROTCE during its investor day in May 2024.
sections.Risks: Recessionary risks. Downside risks include recessionary risks associated with higher than expected inflation, higher-than-expected Fed rate cuts. We believe downside risks weigh higher due to the ongoing trade war. A sharper than expected slowdown in the macroeconomic environment may also lead to slower loan growth and higher delinquencies and credit losses. Execution risks. Execution risks are abound as JPM has been investing heavily in the last few years and in more recent times, embarked on overseas retail expansion as it seeks steadier revenue contributions. JPM continues to work on Chase UK, and is reportedly targeting to launch in Germany, as well as several other European countries by 2030. Jia Hui NG Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Reels and AI to drive long-term growth

sections.Company Overview: Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) operates as a social technology company. META builds applications and technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. META operates under two segments: Family of Apps (FoA) and Reality Labs (RL). Daily active users in Meta Platforms stand at 3.5bn compared to its major competitor TikTok which has an estimated user base of 1.4bn.
sections.Investment Overview: Reels and AI-driven recommendations remain META’s core growth engine. In 4Q25, Instagram Reels watch time rose over 30% y/y in the US, while Facebook video time grew at a double-digit pace, driven by ranking and product improvements. Ongoing ad system upgrades (Andromeda, GEM, Lattice) continue to lift ad performance, with management citing gains in clicks, conversion rates, and ad quality. AI creation tools are also gaining traction, with nearly 10% of Reels now created using the Edits app, while business messaging and WhatsApp monetization (paid messaging and ads in Status) are scaling as incremental growth drivers. META is set to sharply increase AI infrastructure investment in FY26F. META guided capex of USD115–135bn for FY26F to expand data centers, servers, and network capacity for Meta Superintelligence Labs and core AI workloads. Management is focused on scaling compute, improving efficiency, and maintaining flexibility across silicon and infrastructure as it advances Llama and embeds AI more deeply across ads, recommendations, and consumer products. While this will pressure near-term spending, these investments should strengthen long-term AI leadership and support continued product and monetization gains. Reality Labs remains a long-term option on AI-powered wearables and next- gen computing. In 4Q25, revenue fell 12% y/y due to tough comps and channel timing, but smart glasses sales more than tripled last year. The Ray-Ban Meta glasses now integrate AI features such as hands-free assistance and real-time translation, highlighting progress toward always-on, AI-native devices. Most Reality Labs investment is now focused on glasses and wearables, with VR positioned as a longer-term ecosystem play. We maintain BUY with a revised TP of USD1,000 (from USD913). Our TP is based on a 12-month forward PE of 33x (prev 31x) vs peer average of 37x, on a blended net profit of USD78bn. We narrowed the peer discount to 10% (from 15%) on better earnings CAGR prospects and a positive market reaction to stronger 1Q26F revenue guidance and a 59–87% y/y jump in FY26F AI capex. Consensus sees 17% earnings CAGR versus peers’ 20% over FY26F-28F.
sections.Risks: Key risks include margin pressure from AI and infrastructure spending, regulatory scrutiny, and reputational risks from content policy changes. Sachin MittaL Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Beneficiary of AI-driven growth in memory and storage demand

sections.Company Overview: Micron Technology, Inc. is a provider of memory and storage solutions. The company's product portfolio includes dynamic random-access memory (DRAM), negative-AND (NAND), three- dimensional (3D) XPoint memory, NOR, solid-state drives, graphics, high bandwidth memory (HBM), managed NAND, and multichip packages. Its brands include Micron and Crucial. The company's segments include its Compute and Networking Business Unit (CNBU), which covers memory products sold to the client, and the cloud server, enterprise, graphics, and networking markets; and its Mobile Business Unit (MBU), which includes memory and storage products sold to the smartphone and other mobile device markets. DRAM accounted for 70% of the company's total revenue in FY24 while NAND accounted for 29%.
sections.Investment Overview: MU is one of the leaders in NAND technology. Its 1-alpha DRAM and 176-layer NAND ramps are several quarters ahead of the industry and progressing well, as the company continues to qualify new products that use these nodes. NAND memory (3D chip) is in high demand. Since NAND does not need power, it is cost-effective per byte with high storage capacity and is easily replaceable. It is ideal for use in mobile devices, data centres, and automobiles, and for Internet of Things applications. It is one of the components essential for AI adoption, which is expected to see robust growth in the next decade. Data centre growth is an important tailwind, given the digital transformation of enterprises' on-site IT to the cloud. The data centre market is forecasted to grow at a rapid 22.0% CAGR from 2021 to 2026. Big Tech names' cloud services, like Amazon Web Services (AWS) of Amazon, Azure of Microsoft, and Google Cloud, have become the fastest growing parts of their businesses. According to its own estimates, Micron expects a rapid 28% CAGR in DRAM and 33% CAGR in NAND for data centre applications up until 2025. Autonomous driving also requires over 30 times more DRAM and over 100 times more NAND as it moves from Level 0 to Level 5 or full automation. Micron's own estimates for automotive DRAM growth indicate a blistering 40% CAGR and 49% CAGR in NAND up until 2025. Prices for memory have bottomed in 1H23 and has improved further in 2024. We expect the memory segment to lead the anticipated strong growth of 16.8% y-o-y in overall semiconductor revenue for the industry in 2024. Micron projects demand growth CAGRs of mid-teens in DRAM and low-20s percentage range in NAND over the next few years. Expecting a strong FY25, driven by demand for AI PCs, AI phones, and continued data center AI growth. Over 80% of Micron's DRAM production and over 90% of NAND production is on leading-edge nodes. Micron is ramping next-gen DRAM and NAND nodes, with volume production planned for 2025. This creates a favorable setup for a substantial revenue record with improved profitability going forward and to support long-term growth for the group. Overall, industry supply in 2024 is expected to be below demand for both DRAM and NAND. Maintain BUY with higher TP of US$149.00 per share. Our TP is pegged to 3.5x forward PB, (vs 4.2x previously) which is still below peers’ average of c.5-6x, given the improving outlook. Micron is well-positioned to be a major beneficiary of multi-year AI-driven growth in memory and storage demand from data centers to the edge.
sections.Risks: High volatility in product ASP and demand from consumer electronics.

headline: Intelligent Cloud + Gaming led growth

sections.Company Overview: Microsoft Corp (MSFT) develops, manufactures, licenses, & supports a wide range of software products. It has transformed from a component driven (PC, server) to one driven by the need for cloud capacity. Its Azure platform is thriving & is placed 2nd in public cloud in terms of market share. MSFT has three businesses - Productivity & Business Processes (Office, LinkedIn, Dynamics), Intelligent Cloud (Server Products & Cloud Services, Azure, and Enterprise Services) & More Personal Computing (Windows, Devices, Gaming, Search).
sections.Investment Overview: Microsoft is the global leader in productivity software. This is supported by a comprehensive product ecosystem, strong enterprise relationships, and entrenched market presence. The cloud-based productivity software market is forecast to grow from USD57bn in 2024 to USD129bn by 2030 (14.6% CAGR), with consensus expecting MSFT’s Productivity and Business Processes segment to grow at a 15% CAGR over FY26F–28F. Azure is the primary growth driver. Intelligent Cloud revenue is expected to grow at a 22.5% CAGR over FY26F–28F and account for 41% of FY26F revenue. MSFT holds a 23% cloud market share, with industry-leading cloud margins (42% in FY25) versus 26% in the PC segment, supporting further margin expansion as workloads shift to cloud. Copilot adoption is accelerating, with 100m+ monthly active users (MAUs) and strong enterprise uptake, while Azure AI Foundry is scaling rapidly across 14,000+ customers. Activision strengthens the PC segment and gaming ecosystem. The acquistion of Activision contributed ~3ppt to FY24 revenue growth. Despite near-term PC weakness, higher-margin growth drivers such as Game Pass (+45% y/y) and cloud gaming are gaining traction. BUY MSFT, TP USD678 (prev USD600), based on 37x 12m forward blended earnings of USD136bn. The premium to peers reflects superior earnings growth (17% CAGR vs peers at 13%), driven by accelerating Copilot monetization, deeper AI integration across the enterprise stack, and sustained Azure-led cloud growth.
sections.Risks: Enterprise application spending is cyclical and discretionary, making Microsoft exposed to IT budget cuts during economic slowdowns as application upgrades and migrations are often deferred. Key downside risks include weaker-than-expected cloud growth and spend, FX headwinds, macro deterioration, hiring constraints, intensifying competition, and sharper-than- expected declines in the PC and server markets. Sachin Mittal Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Bumpy road

sections.Company Overview: NIKE Inc. is engaged in the design, marketing, and distribution of athletic footwear, apparel, equipment and accessories and services for sports and fitness activities. The Company's key operating geographies include North America, Europe, Middle East & Africa, Greater China, Asia Pacific & Latin America. It sells a line of equipment and accessories under the NIKE brand, and also designs products specifically for the Jordan brand and Converse brand. The Jordan brand predominantly designs, distributes and licenses athletic and casual footwear, apparel, and accessories that focus on basketball performance, while the Converse brand mainly designs, distributes, and licenses casual sneakers, apparel, and accessories.
sections.Investment Overview: The revitalization of Nike. Nike Direct, especially Nike Brand Digital, has seen a slowdown in growth in both digital and physical channels due to the lack of alluring product launches and effective marketing in the recent year, which has led to greater focus on promotions. For example, Nike's digital platform has delivered a 50/50 split between full-price and promotional sales, which has impacted the overall marketplace and the profitability of Nike's partners. Nike is actively reaching out to its distribution partners globally to deliver a strong product pipeline and investments in the channels. Partnership for NikeSKIMS. Nike's latest partnership with Kim Kardashian for "NikeSKIMS" targets women's fitness and activewear, with the first collection to debut in Spring 2025 and global expansion to follow in 2026. While near-term earnings impact is limited, the partnership demonstrates a breakthrough into the womenswear market where there is growing demand for premium-quality, stylish designs. Creating space for seasonal launch in fall and holiday season. Nike has been liquidating excess inventory and providing greater wholesale discounts to drive volume across NIKE factory stores, and reducing the supply of classic footwear franchises across its summer season category. Nike will increase its brand marketing activities to support key product launches and sports moments. BUY at USD85. Nike has taken action to refresh its line-up of new footwear below USD100 as well as planning to scale new performance and lifestyle models ahead. The Company is also managing expenses tightly through the product cycle transitions, with allocation of resources to maximize consumer impact. Maintain BUY with TP at USD85, based on unchanged 35x FYMay26E PE on the back of recovering earnings.
sections.Risks: Intense market competition. The market continues to face fierce competition from both established players and emerging labels. Supply chain vulnerabilities. With pending tariff risks, Nike is subjected to risks from geopolitical tensions, labour issues, shipping delays and more. Inventory management. Nike has been struggling with inventory management challenges. Any prolonged inventory issue could have a longer than expected impact on its sales trend. Analysts: Alison FOK | dbsvhk@dbs.com Mavis HUI | dbsvhk@dbs.com

headline: Outlook cut despite weight-loss surge offsetting margin drag

sections.Company Overview: Novo Nordisk A/S is a Denmark-based pharmaceutical company engaged in diabetes care. The company operates through two business segments: diabetes and obesity care, and biopharmaceuticals. The diabetes and obesity care segment (94% of FY24 sales) covers insulin, GLP-1, oral anti-diabetic drugs. The biopharmaceuticals segment (6% of FY24 sales) covers hemophilia care, growth hormone therapy and hormone replacement therapy. The company focuses on the US (58% of FY24 sales) and European (21% of FY24 sales) market.
sections.Investment Overview: Obesity care drug growth and cost control drove EPS beat and double-digit revenue gains. In 1H25, diluted EPS grew 23% y/y to DKK12.49, while revenue rose 18% y/y at constant exchange rates to DKK154.9bn. Operating profit advanced 29% y/y to DKK72.2bn, aided by efficiency gains, favourable product mix and international scaling. Obesity care drug sales surged 58% globally, with sales from the US up 17% and international markets expanding 19% as supply constraints eased and launches broadened. GLP-1 diabetes products also delivered strong growth of 10%, helping offset US pricing headwinds. Outlook reflects strong global momentum but acknowledges US demand risks and intensifying competition. Management narrowed FY25 guidance to sales growth of 8% to 14% and operating profit growth of 10% to 16% (both at CER), down from prior 13% to 21% and 16% to 24% ranges. The revision reflects softer-than-anticipated Wegovy uptake in the US and pressure from compounded, unapproved GLP-1 alternatives now used by c.1mn patients, alongside mounting competition from Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 portfolio. Management pointed to opportunities from the planned 2026 US launch of oral semaglutide for obesity and ongoing capacity investments, while also emphasising efficiency measures and pipeline reprioritisation to defend margins. Catalysts coming to an end. Riding on its first-in-class advantage, Novo’s strategy has been to be the best in the market. However, in June 2025, Novo released phase 3 data for its next- generation obesity drug CagriSema, revealing average weight loss of 22.7% over 68 weeks – falling short of the anticipated 25% target. This caused its stock price to fall c.10%. With the disappointing trial results, as well as headwinds from growing competition from compounded drugs, and a new CEO effective 7 August, concerns over the company’s strategic direction have heightened, with its share price faling c.45% YTD. We have a HOLD rating on Novo Nordisk, with TP of DKK340. Our TP is based on 11.6x 25F PE, which is slightly lower than the industry median of 14.3x. We believe its share price would be stagnant due to competitors catching up to its clinical potential and catalysts coming to an end, likely leading to loss in market share and an end of its meteoric rise.
sections.Risks: A large influx of rivals may threaten market share. Many pharma companies are competing for their slice of GLP-1 pie, which is currently controlled by Novo Nordisk with more than half of the market share. Novo is at risk of being knocked off the throne as rivals from around the world demonstrate superior clinical effectiveness with more convenient administration routes at comparable prices. Analyst: Nico Chen | dbsvhk@dbs.com

headline: Growth powered by market-leading, best-in-class GPUs

sections.Company Overview: NVIDIA Corporation is a full-stack computing infrastructure company. The Company accelerates computing to help solve the computational problems. The Company’s segments include Compute & Networking and Graphics. Compute & Networking segment includes its data center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence (AI), cockpit, autonomous driving development agreements and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software.
sections.Investment Overview: Long-term, structural demand for AI applications becoming more critical. Nvidia commands a leading and dominant market share in the AI-chips. GPUs designed and built by Nvidia for the data centers are cutting edge and market leading and preferred to support high-powered computing and in the areas of artificial intelligence, analytics, machine learning and scientific computing. Nvidia’s relentless pursuit towards high-powered computing is key to its growth; this is affirmed by its plan to upgrade its AI accelerators every year, as communicated in latest GTC Conference, with the upcoming Blackwell chips to replace the current Hopper generation. Drivers in place to support elevated growth, going forward. Nvidia, the market leader for AI- chips, has and continues to break new heights with each passing quarter. Latest 2QFY26 results and 3QFY26 guidance are cases in point, as revenues came in line with consensus' estimates even without H20 shipments. Sustained AI/DC CAPEX spending and ongoing Blackwell ramp underpin the strongest sequential q/q uplift in revenue guidance of USD9bn (to USD54bn) in the coming 3QFY26 quarter - with demand for its Hopper and Blackwell AI- chips likely to outstrip supply well in FY2026. While uncertainty over Chinese market revenues remains, management has identified a potential USD2-5bn uplift to Q3FY26 revenues if H20 shipment resumes, subject to government licensing and customers’ orders. Strong fundamentals to support next phase of growth. Nvidia's market leading position is exhibited through its ability to maintain above-peers’ margins for its offerings. Management expects gross margins to revert to the mid-70s range in FY26, after slight dips in coming quarter(s) on Blackwell-ramp. Its net cash position as well as diverse sources of revenue and earnings (i.e., across business segments, customers, geographical markets) are seen as further positives for the stock. We maintain our BUY call with a higher target price of USD220. We raise our TP for Nvidia to USD220 (from USD180), pegged to a near-peak blended FY26F/27F forward P/BV valuation peg of 25x (current 21x) – or above +1 SD over its 3-year average. Sustained AI CAPEX from hyperscalers and sovereign AI buildout underpin robust AI/DC execution and momentum is reflected in its stellar financials, as it is poised to exceed USD200bn of sales in FY26 (+56% y/y). Recent withdrawal of the AI diffusion rule, returning access to the Chinese market, and likely exemption from Trump’s 100% semiconductor tariff given its USD500bn US reshoring commitment are positives for this stock. We remain positive on Nvidia as the leading AI-chip leader, though it bears repeating that the bar for Nvidia has been raised higher as the world’s largest stock, with greater expectation to deliver and ride on this AI/DC momentum.
sections.Risks: Increased scrunity and expectation as the world's largest stock. Rising competition from other chipmakers eyeing the AI/ML space, geopolitical risks, slowdown in AI/DC growth momentum, sharper-than-expected semiconductor tariffs.

headline: Long-term strategy is now bearing fruit

sections.Company Overview: Oracle Corp is the second largest software company in the world by revenue as of October 2023. The company provides enterprise software products, such as enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, human capital management (HCM) software, customer relationship management (CRM) software, enterprise performance management (EPM) software, and supply chain management (SCM) software. It also provides cloud services (IaaS plus SaaS) which contribute c.45% of revenue. The company has around 430,000 customers and 8700 employees in 175 countries spanning the Americas, Europe, Asia.
sections.Investment Overview: Oracle is a leading global software company. Founded in 1977, it enjoyed a first-mover advantage in the database technology sector. Since its incorporation, Oracle has expanded through a series of mergers and acquisitions, spending c.USD80bn on more than 150 acquisitions. The aggressive acquisition strategy has helped the company grow very quickly in the past 20 years and has allowed it to have a wider product range. The company has extended its offerings from database, software, and hardware to cloud infrastructure and services. Cloud offerings as the growth engine. Oracle leverages its strong enterprise products and now offers cloud-based products.
