The company's cloud strategy supports its strong growth as the cloud-based subscription model, giving it the edge to provide more flexible/modular offerings, thereby boosting its customer base. Oracle's most popular cloud-based solutions are Fusion ERP Cloud and NetSuite ERP Cloud. These two popular solutions have fared relatively well in the past quarters and registered 20%-30% y/y growth rates. Sustainable growth of SaaS and IaaS revenue. Oracle’s cloud service (SaaS and IaaS) revenue growth rate maintains a rapid growth of c.27% in FY25, supported by 52% y/y growth in IaaS revenue (42% y/y in FY24). In terms of its IaaS market position, Oracle is still one of the smallest players and has a low single-digit percentage market share currently. However, we are already seeing growth slowing down for some of Oracle's major peers in the infrastructure space. We expect strong growth of its SaaS and IaaS revenue to be maintained, as the company can continue to leverage its popular Fusion/NetSuite ERP offerings and cross-sell/up-sell other SaaS and IaaS solutions. Maintain BUY with a higher TP of USD360 (Prev USD300). Our TP is based on c.17x FY26F EV/sales (Prev 14x), supported by robust cloud business growth momentum, increasing AI- cloud contracts, large multi-cloud bookings, and rising RPO. Consensus expects sales CAGR of 18% for FY25-FY27F (May YE) (historic sales CAGR of 9% over FY21-FY25) compared to the peer average CAGR of 11%. We see upside from the stock largely from (i) continuous market share gain in the public cloud sector with its AI niche and (ii) resilient nature of its back-office SaaS products. Potential share price catalysts include (1) stronger-than-expected revenue growth and (2) M&As with strong synergy.
sections.Risks: Cybersecurity incidents and attacks. The occurrence of any disruptive cyber incidents could harm Oracle's business or damage its brand and reputation, leading to the loss of existing customers. Intensifying competition. Oracle participates in a highly competitive environment globally with a wide range of competitors. Intensified competition could create downward pressure on product pricing and margins.

headline: Diversifying beyond crypto

sections.Company Overview: Robinhood principally focuses on developing applications for cash management through ﬁnancial instruments such as stocks, exchange-traded funds, options, and cryptocurrency. It mainly focuses on the US retail market, and has extended brokerage oﬀering in UK and crypto oﬀering in EU. It also oﬀers debit cards; monthly subscription account services with access to deposits and professional research, learning, and education solutions; and retirement accounts to users.
sections.Investment Overview: Pioneer in commission-free brokerage. By being the ﬁrst mover to oﬀer commission-free and easy-to-use trading services, Robinhood has successfully captured many less advanced yet tech-savvy investors. It has become one of the largest retail brokers by customer count with over 26m funded accounts. To deepen client relationships and increase proﬁtability, it has been expanding its product suite by including crypto, cards, retirement accounts, wealth advisory, and banking. The company is also actively expanding overseas. Showcasing strong operating leverage. The company has achieved positive GAAP proﬁt in FY24 as guided, and the prior concerns on proﬁtability have largely faded as it showcased strong operating leverage with adjusted EBITDA margin reaching 50%+. The user-friendly interface and the broader product ecosystem have empowered market share and client wallet gains amid tech stock rally, while international expansion will also bring structural growth to the company. Diversifying beyond crypto. The company is the first mover among leading stockbrokers to offer crypto trading, which accounts for roughly one-third of total transaction-revenue, demonstrating its ability to bring incremental revenue stream via product innovation. The plan to allow retail traders to invest in AI start-ups is another example. We also like the company's execution to broaden its oﬀerings. The good traction in Robinhood Gold subscription enhanced customer loyalty and product adoption, and the fast growth in retirement assets and expected launch of banking service are key to take up a greater share of clients' wallet. The introduction of advisory services is also timely as demand surged amidst heightened uncertainty, which could lead to a more stable revenue stream. BUY with USD165 TP. Our TP is based on 45x FY26F EV/EBITDA, which we think can be justiﬁed by its c.40% CAGR EBITDA growth during FY24-27F, strong momentum in product cross-selling and high lifetime value for clients as switching cost is high.
sections.Risks: Global economic slowdown, prolonged high market interest rates, noticeable contraction in market liquidity, and a substantial correction in global asset valuation. Ken SHIH

headline: Leading e-sport games player with a robust pipeline

sections.Company Overview: Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) is a leading developer and publisher of interactive entertainment for consumers around the globe. The company’s products are designed for console gaming systems, PCs as well as mobile devices. They are delivered through physical retail, digital download, online platforms, and cloud streaming services. The company focuses on publishing a selected number of titles based on internally owned and developed intellectual properties, including NBA 2K25,Grand Theft Auto, Borderlands series etc.
sections.Investment Overview: Legacy titles and eSports games continue to gain momentum. TTWO has strong game franchises including NBA 2K, Grand Theft Auto (GTA), and Red Dead Online. Going ahead, the largest contributors to Net Bookings are expected to be NBA 2K, the Grand Theft Auto series, Toon Blast, our hyper-casual mobile portfolio, Match Factory, Empires & Puzzles, the Red Dead Redemption series, Sid Meier's Civilization 7, and Words with Friends. Consensus expects the company will deliver above-peer average EBITDA growth of 73% over FY25-27F, supported by strong legacy titles and upcoming e-sport games. Strong multi-year pipeline offers substantial long-term upside. The company has a strong track record of launching high-quality titles and is expected to release approximately 20 titles per year for the next several years, through a combination of new and existing franchises like NBA 2K25. Grand Theft Auto VI, Mafia: The Old Country, and Borderlands 4 are some of the most anticipated releases for FY25. We expect the robust pipeline will drive sustainable revenue growth and earnings. Acquisition strategy to expand the addressable market. TTWO has acquired several development studios, including Playdots, Ruffian Games, and HB Studios to further expand its user base and addressable market. Most recently, TTWO’s Rockstar Games acquired Video Games Deluxe, a Sydney-based development studio, in March 2025. The acquisition of new studios will increase its franchises and benefit overseas expansion, boosting revenue growth. We rate BUY on Take-Two Interactive Software with a TP of USD273 (prev USD237) per share. Our TP is based on 29x (prev 31x) forward PE, c.1S.D below its 5-year historical average of 30x. The increase in the target multiple is driven by the strong revenue and EBITDA growth outlook, coupled with a robust pipeline of games. Potential share price catalyst includes (1) stronger than expected performance of newly launched games; (2) earnings upside from acquisition synergies; (3) better than expected international expansion
sections.Risks: Slower than expected monetisation ramp-up of new games. After the relaxation of Covid measures, people are spending more time outdoors which could affect the time spent on games and lead to slower revenue growth. TTWO might incur more marketing expenses and user acquisition costs to promote new games, impacting earnings.

headline: Watch for AI-driven execution milestones

sections.Company Overview: Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, sells, and leases fully electric vehicles and energy-generation and storage systems, and offers services related to its products, such as leasing of electric vehicles and sales of automotive regulatory credits. Its automotive products include the Model 3, Model Y, Model S, and Model X. Currently, the company produces its electric cars at gigafactories in the United States, Germany, and China.
sections.Investment Overview: Leading EV manufacturer. Tesla is a leading global EV manufacturer, backed by its firm market leadership and healthy automotive margins. Tesla's leading share is backed by its economic MOAT in EV charging infrastructure and supercharger network, autonomous driving and other software (e.g., full self- driving aka FSD). 4Q25 earnings beat. Adjusted EPS of USD0.50 exceeded consensus by 10% (- 16% y/y) while revenue was broadly in line (-3% y/y). Vehicle deliveries declined to 418k units (-16% y/y vs 428k estimate). Though, automotive (ex-credits) gross margin surprised positively at 17.2% (vs 13.7% estimate), supported by favourable regional mix and pricing. 2026 execution risk remains. Tesla’s 2026 outlook hinges on progress across its AI-driven platforms (Cybercab, Optimus and in-house AI chips), with key milestones including Cybercab production targeted for April 2026, a full transition of FSD to a subscription model in 1Q26, and conversion of the Fremont Model S/X lines into an Optimus factory. Consensus currently expects auto volumes to recover to ~1.8mn units in 2026 (+8.2% y/y), though we see downside risk amid easing EV demand, intensifying competition in China, and Tesla’s conversion of EV production toward Optimus. Maintain USD450 TP. Our TP is based on FY26 earnings, using a target PE of 200x (or 2.5SD above historical mean). Tesla’s pivot toward AI provides a long- term growth foundation, but near-term performance will remain sensitive to progress on AI-driven execution milestones.
sections.Risks: Firstly, weaker-than-expected volume sales can lead to downside risks to Tesla's earnings, especially as EV competition heats up, noting rising competition and impressive EV sales growth from China OEM leaders such as BYD. Furthermore, the weakening macroeconomic environment in global/China markets can also pose headwinds to volume sales. Secondly, margin dilution remains as a risk to Tesla, especially amid the ongoing aggressive price cuts in the OEM industry. Elizabelle Pang Analyst groupresearch@dbs.com

headline: Brighter prospects on margin upside

sections.Company Overview: United Airlines (UAL) provides scheduled air transportation for passengers (>90% of total revenue) and cargo (3-5%) throughout the United States and across the globe. United Airlines is one of the largest airlines in the world, with a mainline and regional fleet of 826 and 518 aircraft respectively as of December-21. The airline’s core hubs in the US include New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Chicago, and Denver, and as part of the Star Alliance, the airline also has a vast international route network.
sections.Investment Overview: Long-term margin strategy intact. UAL boasts the most extensive international travel network and has higher exposure to less price-sensitive segments like corporate travel and long-haul. The carrier’s strategic focus on its brand-loyal model, higher-margin flying, de-peaked capacity scheduling, and cost-efficient operations should support margin expansion. Structural tailwinds for stronger pricing and margins. UAL is anticipated to outperform peers amid the strengthening pricing environment in FY26, underpinned by rationalisation of industry supply growth and acceleration in corporate traffic recovery. We also see improving unit costs ahead on the back of better utilisation and higher capacity growth, with cost-cutting initiatives bringing unit costs (excluding fuel) to 8% lower in 2026 vs 2019. Consequently, UAL's operating margin is forecasted to return to pre-pandemic levels of above 10% in the medium-term. Deleveraging remains key. UAL's priority remains to pare down debt to more sustainable levels, given its goal of repairing its balance sheet to its pre-crisis state by 2026, and the group seems to be on track (net leverage at 2.0x). While capex guidance had been lowered previously, management now anticipates a modest near-term uptick as Boeing narrow-body deliveries improve, which could moderate the pace of debt reduction. We believe more opportunistic buybacks will continue upon achieving its leverage target (USD1.5bn share buyback announced in Oct-24). Maintain BUY with a higher TP of USD130.0, based on 5.7x forward EV/EBITDA. UAL is currently trading at a discount to other North American network airlines, and we believe it is largely unwarranted given that its operating margins could surprise on the upside and its capital spending is now moderated in a way that could maximise returns to shareholders.
sections.Risks: Macroeconomic weakness could curb discretionary spending on travel while persistent cost inflation (staff, jet fuel prices etc.) could weigh on margins. Tabitha Foo | Jason Sum

headline: AI-powered credit engine

sections.Company Overview: UPST is a financial technology company that provides AI-powered lending platform for personal loans. Its platform enables banks and other lending institutions to offer loans to consumers with little or no credit history, by using alternative data sources and sophisticated modeling techniques to assess creditworthiness. UPST has partnerships with a number of banks and financial insitutions, and its platform is used to originate loans for a variety of purposes, including debt consolidation, credit card refinancing, and personal expenses.
sections.Investment Overview: AI-powered credit score unlocks lending opportunities. UPST's AI-driven credit score system provides additional loan origination to bank partners. With more feedback and updates during different business cycles, UPST's model improves prediction accuracy and, in turn, improves the conversion rate. The company recently launched one of the most impactful improvements to its model, and has seen 18% of accuracy gains since inception. Repeat customers, loan scale expansion, and new bank outsourcing business (platform fee) should drive UPST's business growth in the long-term period. Rising credit concerns on more in-housed loans. To facilirate expansion into new products like auto/home loans, the company has added more loans to balance sheet for R&D purpose. This, however raised investor concerns amid elevated macro uncertainty. The sequential thinning of fee rate also led to concerns over long-term profitability. Rate cuts tailwind is softening. Rate cut expectations were once a key boost to share price, via lowering its funding costs and supporting a faster growth in originations. Yet, the recent tariff introduction may reignite inflation and adds uncertainty to the rate cut decisions. The management currently incorporates no rate cuts in its guidance. HOLD rating with USD70TP. Our TP is based on 24 FY26F forward EV/EBITDA, in line with historical average, as we think the good traction in new product expansion and the strength in AI-led underwriting model offset the risk from macro weakness.
sections.Risks: Upside risks include consumer credit quality improving faster than expected, consumer demand for unsecured loans improving despite a challenging environment, and institutional and bank funding being more resilient than expected. Downside risks include deterioration of customer credit profile and stricter regulatory supervision. Analysts: Edmond Fok | dbsvhk@dbs.com Manyi Lu | dbsvhk@dbs.com

headline: Cautious consumers driving market share gains

sections.Company Overview: Walmart Inc. offers shopping opportunities in both retail stores and through e-commerce. The Company offers an assortment of merchandise and services at everyday low prices. The Company operates through three segments: Walmart U.S., Walmart International and Sam's Club. The Walmart U.S. segment is a merchandiser of consumer products. The Walmart International segment includes various formats divided into two categories: retail and wholesale. These categories consist of various formats, including supercenters, supermarkets, hypermarkets, warehouse clubs and cash & carry, as well as e-commerce through walmart.com.mx, walmart.ca, flipkart.com and other sites. The Sam's Club segment is a membership-only warehouse club.
sections.Investment Overview: Online sales and technology development to improve shopping experience. Walmart continues to focus on online sales expansion with its well-established delivery service network covering 70% households in the US, with drone delivery covering seven states, while the company is also expanding its GoLocal services via the cooperations with renowned platforms such as Home Depot and Cognery. Online margin is also improving, In the meantime, Walmart's investments in supply chain automation should start to bear fruits and increase overall labour productivity. Walmart’s own ecosystem to be a key differentiator. Walmart has expanded its businesses from grocery retailing and general merchandises to tap into healthcare and financial services for its customers, allowing it to create stronger bonding and diversify its revenue and profit sources. Walmart should also achieve a faster momentum in its international markets with high-single digit sales growth. Margin improvement. Walmart's gross margin continues to benefit from more favourable business mix as the company expands its presense in e-commerce and advertising in both US and international markets. Walmart's pricing strategies could potentially attract more affluent shoppers and expand its customer base. With the ongoing improvement in supply-chain and staff management, Walmart should also be able achieve better operating efficiency and margin growth over the medium-term. We rate BUY on Walmart with a TP of USD120. Our TP is based on 41x FY26 PE in view of Walmart's continuing share gains given the cautious consumer sentiment as well as its ongoing operating efficiency improvement.
sections.Risks: Rising competition; prolonged geopolitical uncertainties and inflation; slowdown in e- commerce growth; significant increase in capex to support e-commerce and technological advancement; etc. Analysts: Mavis HUI | dbsvhk@dbs.com Clement XU | dbsvhk@dbs.com
